english: rected accordingly. — 4. with gin (gin) (B. and col.) and čin (cin) (B.), denoting a continued action, state, or condition, as in English: I am looking. — °dúg-gnas ('dug gnas), °dúg-sa ('dug sa), place of residence, abode.

pf. btud (btud), fut. gdud (gdud) (Cs.), imp. dud (dud), tud (thud) (Cs.), to bend or bow down, to incline, rná-ba (rna ba), to incline one's ears to hear, (also used of animals), cf. our 'to prick the ears', Dzl.; to bow, to make a bow, la (la), to a person; żábs-la (zhabs la) at a person's feet, to kneel down before a person.
go-°dún (go 'dun), = sna-tsogs (sna tshogs) of several kinds, divers, sundry, various, Lex.

1. vb. to desire, to wish earnestly, with la (la), nyán-pa-la mi °dún-par (nyan pa la mi 'dun par) they not having any desire to hear Pth.; dgé-ba-la (dge ba la) to strive after virtue, frq.; also °dún-pa ('dun pa) alone (without dgé-ba-la (dge ba la)) id. Thg.; *lo čhó-la °dúm-pa* (lo chos la 'dum pa) C. religious interest, concern for religion; to be zealous, to take a warm interest Mil. — 2. sbst. a desire Thgy.; a supplication Dzl., Glr. Cf. dún-pa (dun pa).

1. advice, counsel, ṅán-pa (ngan pa) a bad advice Ma.; °débs-pa ('debs pa) to give advice; byéd-pa (byed pa) to take a resolution Mil. — 2. consultation (v. examples sub čúṅ-ba (chung ba)), °dun-grós ('dun gros) id.; da-lán-gyi °dun-grós °dí-la (da lan gyi 'dun gros 'di la) at this present consultation Glr.; *°dúm-ma ǰhé'-pa* ('dum ma jhe' pa) C. to consult, to confer with (a person about a matter). — 3. council, °dún-mar bsdus ('dun mar bsdus) they called a council together Mil.; esp. in compounds: °dún-kań ('dun khang) = °dú-kań ('du khang) q.v.; °dún-sa ('dun sa) meeting-place, assembly, frq.; union, association, society, dge-°dun (dge 'dun) an association of clerical persons. — 4. v. °dum ('dum)? gnyen-°dun (gnyen 'dun) harmony amongst relations, Stg. — 5. the state of being a bride; bride, C., and perh. Glr.; cf. also dga-°dún (dga' 'dun) sub dgá-ba (dga' ba). — 6. = mdún-ma (mdun ma)?
Sch. a state of comfort, ease; °dub-khrúgs ('dub khrugs), an interruption of that state, discomfort.

1. vb. to reconcile one's self to, to be reconciled with, táb-pa °dúm-na (thab pa 'dum na) if contending parties are reconciled with one another; rtág-tu mi °dúm-mo (rtag tu mi 'dum mo) they are constantly at variance Dzl.; *°dúm-ṭa* ('dum khra) (lit. kra (khra)) C. contract, agreement, = čad-don (chad don). — 2. sbst. concord, unison, peace Cs.

I. vb., pf. btul (btul), tul (thul), fut. gdul (gdul), imp. tul (thul), W. *túl-če* (thul ce) 1. to tame, to break in, rta (rta); to subdue, conquer, vanquish, dgra (dgra); sometimes even to kill, to annihilate Pth. — 2. to till, cultivate, waste land; to civilize, a nation, which with the Buddhist is the same as to convert, frq.; to educate, to discipline, to punish; gdúl-bai rigs-pa (gdul ba'i rigs pa) those fit for and predestinated to conversion Dzl.; gdúl-bya (gdul bya) id. frq.; also used substantively: gró-ba ṅá-yi gdúl-bya yin ('gro ba nga yi gdul bya yin) the beings are to be converted by me Glr.; bdag kyéḍ-kyi gdúl-byar śog čig (bdag khyed kyi gdul byar shog cig) may we become your converts! II. sbst. 1. the taming etc. — 2. also °dúl-bai sde ('dul ba'i sde), the disciplinary part of the Kangyur, °dul-ba-las ('dul ba las) from, or according to the Dulwa; °dúl-bai brda ('dul ba'i brda) an expression (taken) from the Dulwa.

pf. bteg(s) (bteg(s)), fut. gdeg (gdeg), imp. teg (theg), W. *tág-če* (tag ce), imp. *tog* (tog), to lift, to raise, to elevate, the head, the tail, also fig.; sgrón-me (sgron me) Glr., *°od-ṭo* ('od khro) W., to hold up a lamp, a light; also fig.: to let one's light shine to others; grágs-pai gó-sar °dégs-pa (grags pa'i go sar 'degs pa) to raise to a high rank; to support, sustain, maintain, keep up, Pth.; rám-bu °dégs-pa (ram bu 'degs pa) to join in singing, to fall in with, Dzl. and elsewh. (Sch. erron. 'to bawl, to blare'); rá-mda °dégs-pa (ra mda' 'degs pa) to help; for *żi tág-če* (zhi tag ce) and similar phrases cf. the secondary forms tég-pa (theg pa), tégs-pa (thegs pa), ťégs-pa (thegs pa); with or without sráṅ-la (srang la), rgyá-ma-la (rgya ma la) etc.: to put on the balance, to weigh, B.; żib-btégs (zhib btegs) weighed accurately
